title: The Song of Purrav
summary: Krishna teaches Uddhava about witness consciousness, detachment from family/objects, and devotion (Bhakti). Addresses relationship endings, dealing with grief, letting go of attachments, and mental calm.

sections.summary: This passage begins with “The Supreme Lord said: 'Having acquired this human body that is My characteristic, one achieves, being situated in My dharma, Me, the Supreme Soul of Spiritual Happiness situated in the…” and develops toward “One can never tell whether this body belongs to one's parents, wife or employer, to [the funeral] fire, the dogs and jackals or to the [indwelling]…”

This passage begins with “In what sense would one differ from worms when one enjoys that what is composed of skin, flesh, blood, muscle, fat, marrow and bone, urine, stool and pus?” and develops toward “He [Purrav] who for that reason no longer desired the world of Urvas', thus liberated from all attachment, innerly satisfied wandered this earth.'”

verses.sanskrit: श्री-भगवान् उवाच मल्-लक्षणम् इमं कायं लब्ध्वा मद्-धर्म आस्थितः । आनन्दं परमात्मानम् आत्म-स्थं समुपैति माम्
verses.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvāca mal-lakṣaṇam imaṃ kāyaṃ labdhvā mad-dharma āsthitaḥ / ānandaṃ paramātmānam ātma-sthaṃ samupaiti mām
verses.translation: The Supreme Lord said: 'Having acquired this human body that is My characteristic, one achieves, being situated in My dharma, Me, the Supreme Soul of Spiritual Happiness situated in the heart.

verses.sanskrit: गुण-मय्या जीव-योन्या विमुक्तो ज्ञान-निष्ठया । गुणेषु माया-मात्रेषु दृश्यमानेष्व् अवस्तुतः । वर्तमानो ऽपि न पुमान् युज्यते ऽवस्तुभिर् गुणैः
verses.transliteration: guṇa-mayyā jīva-yonyā vimukto jñāna-niṣṭhayā / guṇeṣu māyā-mātreṣu dṛśyamāneṣv avastutaḥ / vartamāno 'pi na pumān yujyate 'vastubhir guṇaiḥ
verses.translation: Someone who, fixed in spiritual knowledge, has become free from the cause of material life based on the products of the modes, does not get entangled in their illusory qualities despite of being surrounded by them; although present before his eyes they are insubstantial and nothing but illusion to him.

verses.sanskrit: सङ्गं न कुर्याद् असतां शिश्नोदर-तृपां क्वचित् । तस्यानुगस् तमस्य् अन्धे पतत्य् अन्धानुगान्ध-वत्
verses.transliteration: saṅgaṃ na kuryād asatāṃ śiśnodara-tṛpāṃ kvacit / tasyānugas tamasy andhe pataty andhānugāndha-vat
verses.translation: One should never at any time seek the company of materialists devoted to the gratification of their genitals and bellies, for they who follow such people will fall into the darkest pit, like a blind man following another one.

verses.sanskrit: ऐलः सम्राड् इमां गाथाम् अगायत बृहच्-छ्रवाः । उर्वशी-विरहान् मुह्यन् निर्विण्णः शोक-संयमे
verses.transliteration: ailaḥ samrāḍ imāṃ gāthām agāyata bṛhac-chravāḥ / urvaśī-virahān muhyan nirviṇṇaḥ śoka-saṃyame
verses.translation: The descendant of Il [called Aila or Purrav, see also 9.14: 15-16], the well-known great emperor, sang the following mighty song when he, bewildered being separated from Urvas', in resignation managed to restrain his grief.

verses.sanskrit: त्यक्त्वात्मानं व्रयन्तीं तां नग्न उन्मत्त-वन् नृपः । विलपन्न् अन्वगाज् जाये घोरे तिष्ठेति विक्लवः
verses.transliteration: tyaktvātmānaṃ vrayantīṃ tāṃ nagna unmatta-van nṛpaḥ / vilapann anvagāj jāye ghore tiṣṭheti viklavaḥ
verses.translation: The moment she abandoned him and left, he like a madman naked and crying ran after her calling out: 'Oh my wife, oh you terrible woman, please stop!'

verses.sanskrit: कामान् अतृप्तो ऽनुजुषन् क्षुल्लकान् वर्ष-यामिनीः । न वेद यान्तीर् नायान्तीर् उर्वश्य्-आकृष्ट-चेतनः
verses.transliteration: kāmān atṛpto 'nujuṣan kṣullakān varṣa-yāminīḥ / na veda yāntīr nāyāntīr urvaśy-ākṛṣṭa-cetanaḥ
verses.translation: With his mind possessed by Urvas' he, after years of insignificantly gratifying his lusts, was not satisfied and did not notice the nights coming and going.'

verses.sanskrit: ऐल उवाच अहो मे मोह-विस्तारः काम-कश्मल-चेतसः । देव्या गृहीत-कण्ठस्य नायुः-खण्डा इमे स्मृताः
verses.transliteration: aila uvāca aho me moha-vistāraḥ kāma-kaśmala-cetasaḥ / devyā gṛhīta-kaṇṭhasya nāyuḥ-khaṇḍā ime smṛtāḥ
verses.translation: Purrav said: 'Just see how bewildered I got! With my consciousness contaminated by lust I, in the embrace of this goddess, did not notice my life time passing.

verses.sanskrit: नाहं वेदाभिनिर्मुक्तः सूर्यो वाभ्युदितो ऽमुया । मूषितो वर्ष-पूगानां बताहानि गतान्य् उत
verses.transliteration: nāhaṃ vedābhinirmuktaḥ sūryo vābhyudito 'muyā / mūṣito varṣa-pūgānāṃ batāhāni gatāny uta
verses.translation: Captivated by her I had no idea whether the sun was rising or setting and have thus alas, for many years, spent my days.

verses.sanskrit: अहो मे आत्म-सम्मोहो येनात्मा योषितां कृतः । क्रीडा-मृगश् चक्रवर्ती नरदेव-शिखामणिः
verses.transliteration: aho me ātma-sammoho yenātmā yoṣitāṃ kṛtaḥ / krīḍā-mṛgaś cakravartī naradeva-śikhāmaṇiḥ
verses.translation: Oh what a pity this total bewilderment of mine because of which the body of this mighty emperor, this crown jewel of kings, became a toy animal for women!

verses.sanskrit: स-परिच्छदम् आत्मानं हित्वा तृणम् इवेश्वरम् । यान्तीं स्त्रियं चान्वगमं नग्न उन्मत्त-वद् रुदन्
verses.transliteration: sa-paricchadam ātmānaṃ hitvā tṛṇam iveśvaram / yāntīṃ striyaṃ cānvagamaṃ nagna unmatta-vad rudan
verses.translation: When she abandoned me, the mighty controller, together with all of my kingdom as if I were a blade of grass, I ran crying naked like a madman after the woman.

verses.sanskrit: कुतस् तस्यानुभावः स्यात् तेज ईशत्वम् एव वा । यो ऽन्वगच्छं स्त्रियं यान्तीं खर-वत् पाद-ताडितः
verses.transliteration: kutas tasyānubhāvaḥ syāt teja īśatvam eva vā / yo 'nvagacchaṃ striyaṃ yāntīṃ khara-vat pāda-tāḍitaḥ
verses.translation: Where is now the influence, strength and sovereignty of the person I am? I ran after this woman leaving me, just like an ass being kicked with the hoof for punishment!

verses.sanskrit: किं विद्यया किं तपसा किं त्यागेन श्रुतेन वा । किं विविक्तेन मौनेन स्त्रीभिर् यस्य मनो हृतम्
verses.transliteration: kiṃ vidyayā kiṃ tapasā kiṃ tyāgena śrutena vā / kiṃ viviktena maunena strībhir yasya mano hṛtam
verses.translation: What is the use of knowledge, austerities, renunciation, the scriptures, or of solitude and silence, for the one whose mind is stolen by women?

verses.sanskrit: स्वार्थस्याकोविदं धिङ् मां मूर्खं पण्डित-मानिनम् । यो ऽहम् ईश्वरतां प्राप्य स्त्रीभिर् गो-खर-वज् जितः
verses.transliteration: svārthasyākovidaṃ dhiṅ māṃ mūrkhaṃ paṇḍita-māninam / yo 'ham īśvaratāṃ prāpya strībhir go-khara-vaj jitaḥ
verses.translation: To hell with the fool I am unaware of what is good for him; I who thought to be a scholar in achieving the position of a lord and controller but who, just like a bullock or ass, was conquered by women!

verses.sanskrit: सेवतो वर्ष-पूगान् मे उर्वश्या अधरासवम् । न तृप्यत्य् आत्म-भूः कामो वह्निर् आहुतिभिर् यथा
verses.transliteration: sevato varṣa-pūgān me urvaśyā adharāsavam / na tṛpyaty ātma-bhūḥ kāmo vahnir āhutibhir yathā
verses.translation: For so many years serving Urvas''s lips, I, with the lust born from my mind, never got enough of the nectar, just like a fire one can never satisfy with oblations.

verses.sanskrit: पुंश्चल्यापहृतं चित्तं को न्व् अन्यो मोचितुं प्रभुः । आत्मारामेश्वरम् ऋते भगवन्तम् अधोक्षजम्
verses.transliteration: puṃścalyāpahṛtaṃ cittaṃ ko nv anyo mocituṃ prabhuḥ / ātmārāmeśvaram ṛte bhagavantam adhokṣajam
verses.translation: Who else but the Innerly Satisfied Lord of the Sages, the Supreme Lord Beyond the Senses, can free someone who lost his mind with a courtesan?

verses.sanskrit: बोधितस्यापि देव्या मे सूक्त-वाक्येन दुर्मतेः । मनो-गतो महा-मोहो नापयात्य् अजितात्मनः
verses.transliteration: bodhitasyāpi devyā me sūkta-vākyena durmateḥ / mano-gato mahā-moho nāpayāty ajitātmanaḥ
verses.translation: Out of control with myself being dull-minded, I saw no end to my confusion, even though the goddess [Urvas'] eloquently gave me advice [see 9.14: 20-22].

verses.sanskrit: किम् एतया नो ऽपकृतं रज्ज्वा वा सर्प-चेतसः । द्रष्टुः स्वरूपाविदुषो यो ऽहं यद् अजितेन्द्रियः
verses.transliteration: kim etayā no 'pakṛtaṃ rajjvā vā sarpa-cetasaḥ / draṣṭuḥ svarūpāviduṣo yo 'haṃ yad ajitendriyaḥ
verses.translation: What would she have done wrong to a 'seer' like me who, taking a rope for a snake, has no notion of his real nature [his svarpa]? I am the one out of control with his senses is it not?

verses.sanskrit: क्वायं मलीमसः कायो दौर्गन्ध्याद्य्-आत्मको ऽशुचिः । क्व गुणाः सौमनस्याद्या ह्य् अध्यासो ऽविद्यया कृतः
verses.transliteration: kvāyaṃ malīmasaḥ kāyo daurgandhyādy-ātmako 'śuciḥ / kva guṇāḥ saumanasyādyā hy adhyāso 'vidyayā kṛtaḥ
verses.translation: What does this filthy body have to offer, unclean and full of bad odors; what are those 'pleasing [feminine] qualities' and so on anyway? They constitute an influence rooting in ignorance!

verses.sanskrit: पित्रोः किं स्वं नु भार्यायाः स्वामिनो ऽग्नेः श्व-गृध्रयोः । किम् आत्मनः किं सुहृदाम् इति यो नावसीयते
verses.transliteration: pitroḥ kiṃ svaṃ nu bhāryāyāḥ svāmino 'gneḥ śva-gṛdhrayoḥ / kim ātmanaḥ kiṃ suhṛdām iti yo nāvasīyate
verses.translation: One can never tell whether this body belongs to one's parents, wife or employer, to [the funeral] fire, the dogs and jackals or to the [indwelling] soul or one's fr iends. One gets attached to this unholy matter and praises it, in case of a woman, for having such a cute nose, beautiful smile and face, but one heads with it for the lowest destination [of decay].

verses.sanskrit: तस्मिन् कलेवरे ऽमेध्ये तुच्छ-निष्ठे विषज्जते । अहो सु-भद्रं सु-नसं सु-स्मितं च मुखं स्त्रियः
verses.transliteration: tasmin kalevare 'medhye tuccha-niṣṭhe viṣajjate / aho su-bhadraṃ su-nasaṃ su-smitaṃ ca mukhaṃ striyaḥ
verses.translation: One can never tell whether this body belongs to one's parents, wife or employer, to [the funeral] fire, the dogs and jackals or to the [indwelling] soul or one's fr iends. One gets attached to this unholy matter and praises it, in case of a woman, for having such a cute nose, beautiful smile and face, but one heads with it for the lowest destination [of decay].

verses.sanskrit: त्वङ्-मांस-रुधिर-स्नायु- मेदो-मज्जास्थि-संहतौ । विण्-मूत्र-पूये रमतां कृमीणां कियद् अन्तरम्
verses.transliteration: tvaṅ-māṃsa-rudhira-snāyu- medo-majjāsthi-saṃhatau / viṇ-mūtra-pūye ramatāṃ kṛmīṇāṃ kiyad antaram
verses.translation: In what sense would one differ from worms when one enjoys that what is composed of skin, flesh, blood, muscle, fat, marrow and bone, urine, stool and pus?

verses.sanskrit: अथापि नोपसज्जेत स्त्रीषु स्त्रैणेषु चार्थ-वित् । विषयेन्द्रिय-संयोगान् मनः क्षुभ्यति नान्यथा
verses.transliteration: athāpi nopasajjeta strīṣu straiṇeṣu cārtha-vit / viṣayendriya-saṃyogān manaḥ kṣubhyati nānyathā
verses.translation: A man understanding what is best for him, should never run after women or associate with men thus engaged, for the sole reason that the mind, united with the senses, reaches for sense objects and thus gets agitated [compare 5.5: 2, 7.12: 9, 9.19: 17, 9.14: 36].

verses.sanskrit: अदृष्टाद् अश्रुताद् भावान् न भाव उपजायते । असम्प्रयुञ्जतः प्राणान् शाम्यति स्तिमितं मनः
verses.transliteration: adṛṣṭād aśrutād bhāvān na bhāva upajāyate / asamprayuñjataḥ prāṇān śāmyati stimitaṃ manaḥ
verses.translation: [Because] a thing not heard or seen gives no rise to mental agitation, the mind of someone not engaging his senses calms down and finds peace.

verses.sanskrit: तस्मात् सङ्गो न कर्तव्यः स्त्रीषु स्त्रैणेषु चेन्द्रियैः । विदुषां चाप्य् अविस्रब्धः षड्-वर्गः किम् उ मादृशाम्
verses.transliteration: tasmāt saṅgo na kartavyaḥ strīṣu straiṇeṣu cendriyaiḥ / viduṣāṃ cāpy avisrabdhaḥ ṣaḍ-vargaḥ kim u mādṛśām
verses.translation: When not even wise men can rely on the six enemies [lust, anger, greed, bewilderment, intoxication and envy; the shath-varga], then what about persons like me? One therefore should not get sensorily attached to women or to men attached to women [see also yoshita].'

verses.sanskrit: श्री-भगवान् उवाच एवं प्रगायन् नृप-देव-देवः स उर्वशी-लोकम् अथो विहाय । आत्मानम् आत्मन्य् अवगम्य मां वै उपारमज् ज्ञाअन-विधूत-मोहः
verses.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvāca evaṃ pragāyan nṛpa-deva-devaḥ sa urvaśī-lokam atho vihāya / ātmānam ātmany avagamya māṃ vai upāramaj jñāana-vidhūta-mohaḥ
verses.translation: The Supreme Lord said: 'He, the worshipable lord of gods and men, who thus sang his song [of lamentation], then gave up the world of Urvas'. Realizing Me, the Supersoul in his heart, he by the transcendental knowledge found peace within himself and ended his illusion.

verses.sanskrit: ततो दुःसङ्गम् उत्सृज्य सत्सु सज्जेत बुद्धिमान् । सन्त एवास्य छिन्दन्ति मनो-व्यासङ्गम् उक्तिभिः
verses.transliteration: tato duḥsaṅgam utsṛjya satsu sajjeta buddhimān / santa evāsya chindanti mano-vyāsaṅgam uktibhiḥ
verses.translation: An intelligent person having abandoned bad association therefore should be fixed on devotees, for only by their words he can cut with the deep attachment of his mind.

verses.sanskrit: सन्तो ऽनपेक्षा मच्-चित्ताः प्रशान्ताः सम-दर्शिनः । निर्ममा निरहङ्कारा निर्द्वन्द्वा निष्परिग्रहाः
verses.transliteration: santo 'napekṣā mac-cittāḥ praśāntāḥ sama-darśinaḥ / nirmamā nirahaṅkārā nirdvandvā niṣparigrahāḥ
verses.translation: Devotees with their minds fixed on Me do not depend [on lusts] and are, with an equal minded vision, completely peaceful and free from possessiveness, false ego, dualities and greed.

verses.sanskrit: तेषु नित्यं महा-भाग महा-भागेषु मत्-कथाः । सम्भवन्ति हि ता नॄणां जुषतां प्रपुनन्त्य् अघम्
verses.transliteration: teṣu nityaṃ mahā-bhāga mahā-bhāgeṣu mat-kathāḥ / sambhavanti hi tā nṝṇāṃ juṣatāṃ prapunanty agham
verses.translation: Oh most fortunate one, these greatly fortunate souls are constantly discussing My stories that have the power to completely eradicate the sins of anyone choosing for them.

verses.sanskrit: ता ये शृण्वन्ति गायन्ति ह्य् अनुमोदन्ति चादृताः । मत्-पराः श्रद्दधानाश् च भक्तिं विन्दन्ति ते मयि
verses.transliteration: tā ye śṛṇvanti gāyanti hy anumodanti cādṛtāḥ / mat-parāḥ śraddadhānāś ca bhaktiṃ vindanti te mayi
verses.translation: They who, faithfully dedicated to Me, hear, chant and respectfully take them [My stories] to heart, will attain My bhakti.

verses.sanskrit: भक्तिं लब्धवतः साधोः किम् अन्यद् अवशिष्यते । मय्य् अनन्त-गुणे ब्रह्मण्य् आनन्दानुभवात्मनि
verses.transliteration: bhaktiṃ labdhavataḥ sādhoḥ kim anyad avaśiṣyate / mayy ananta-guṇe brahmaṇy ānandānubhavātmani
verses.translation: What else would there remain [to be accomplished] for a devotee once he has achieved devotional service unto Me, the Absolute Truth of Countless Qualities, comprising the experience of spiritual happiness?

verses.sanskrit: यथोपश्रयमाणस्य भगवन्तं विभावसुम् । शीतं भयं तमो ऽप्येति साधून् संसेवतस् तथा
verses.transliteration: yathopaśrayamāṇasya bhagavantaṃ vibhāvasum / śītaṃ bhayaṃ tamo 'pyeti sādhūn saṃsevatas tathā
verses.translation: Just as cold, fear and darkness will dissipate for the one who resorts to the supreme grace of fire [Agni], similarly dullness, apprehension and ignorance will dissolve for someone who serves the devotees.

verses.sanskrit: निमज्ज्योन्मज्जतां घोरे भवाब्धौ परमायणम् । सन्तो ब्रह्म-विदः शान्ता नौर् दृढेवाप्सु मज्जताम्
verses.transliteration: nimajjyonmajjatāṃ ghore bhavābdhau paramāyaṇam / santo brahma-vidaḥ śāntā naur dṛḍhevāpsu majjatām
verses.translation: For those who submerge and again rise in the fearful ocean of material life, the saintly devotees, peacefu l in understanding the Absolute, constitute a supreme shelter as good as a life boat for people drowning in the water [compare 11.23: 28 and 11.17: 44].

verses.sanskrit: अन्नं हि प्राणिनां प्राण आर्तानां शरणं त्व् अहम् । धर्मो वित्तं नृणां प्रेत्य सन्तो ऽर्वाग् बिभ्यतो ऽरणम्
verses.transliteration: annaṃ hi prāṇināṃ prāṇa ārtānāṃ śaraṇaṃ tv aham / dharmo vittaṃ nṛṇāṃ pretya santo 'rvāg bibhyato 'raṇam
verses.translation: Just as food is there to grant the living beings their life, just as I exist as the shelter for the distressed and dharma is there as the wealth of the deceased, so my devotees constitute the refuge of those afraid to fall down.

verses.sanskrit: सन्तो दिशन्ति चक्षूंसि बहिर् अर्कः समुत्थितः । देवता बान्धवाः सन्तः सन्त आत्माहम् एव च
verses.transliteration: santo diśanti cakṣūṃsi bahir arkaḥ samutthitaḥ / devatā bāndhavāḥ santaḥ santa ātmāham eva ca
verses.translation: The devotees grant you the [divine] eyes while the sun [only] shows the external world after having risen; they are the worshipable ones, one's [true] relatives, they are one's actual self and Me as well [see also e.g. 1.1: 15, 3.5: 47, 3.6: 28, 11.2: 6].

verses.sanskrit: वैतसेनस् ततो ऽप्य् एवम् उर्वश्या लोक-निष्पृहः । मुक्त-सङ्गो महीम् एताम् आत्मारामश् चचार ह
verses.transliteration: vaitasenas tato 'py evam urvaśyā loka-niṣpṛhaḥ / mukta-saṅgo mahīm etām ātmārāmaś cacāra ha
verses.translation: He [Purrav] who for that reason no longer desired the world of Urvas', thus liberated from all attachment, innerly satisfied wandered this earth.'
